summary: Jest snapshot tests failed because thrown errors did not match stored snapshots; a SyntaxError from the parser is shown in the test output.
root_cause: Multiple Jest snapshot assertions failed: the received errors (including a SyntaxError from the parser) did not match expected snapshots, causing snapshot test failures.

evidence.reason: Shows the specific Jest assertion that failed (snapshot mismatch on thrown error) repeated many times in the log.
Indicates multiple Jest snapshots failed early in the run.

evidence.quote: SyntaxError: A parameter initializer is only allowed in a function or constructor implementation. (3:17)
evidence.reason: A parser SyntaxError is present in the output; this error likely changed the error text or behavior that snapshots expect.
Stack trace points to parser error creation code in the repository, tying the SyntaxError to project code mentioned in the tests.
Stack trace shows Babel parse error utility produced the error observed by tests, explaining why snapshot of the thrown error did not match.

evidence.quote: › 13 snapshots failed from 2 test suites. Inspect your code changes or re-run jest with `-u` to update them.
evidence.reason: Jest recommends inspecting code changes or running `-u` when snapshots differ, directly relevant to resolving the failures.
Summarizes that a small number of test suites failed while the rest passed, indicating localized snapshot assertion failures rather than a broad test outage.

suggested_fix: Inspect the failing snapshots and the code that produces the parser error; if the new error messages are expected, re-run jest with -u to update snapshots (as suggested by the output: `Inspect your code changes or re-run jest with `-u` to update them.`). Otherwise fix the parser behavior that produces the different SyntaxError so the thrown errors match existing snapshots.
